question: What is the full list of hook events a charm can observe? List them all.

claude_output.result:
Here is the full list of Juju hook events a charm can observe, organized by category:

## Lifecycle Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `install` | Fired once when the unit is first deployed |
| `config-changed` | Fired when charm configuration changes (and on install/upgrade) |
| `start` | Fired after `install` and initial `config-changed` |
| `upgrade-charm` | Fired when the charm code is upgraded |
| `stop` | Fired when the unit is about to be destroyed (deprecated in favor of `remove`) |
| `remove` | Fired when the unit is being removed |
| `update-status` | Fired periodically (every ~5 minutes by default) |
| `collect-unit-status` | Fired before status is reported, to collect unit status |
| `collect-app-status` | Fired before status is reported, to collect app status (leader only) |

## Leader Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `leader-elected` | Fired when the unit becomes the application leader |
| `leader-settings-changed` | Fired on non-leader units when leader data changes |

## Relation Hooks
*(fired per relation endpoint defined in `metadata.yaml`)*
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `relation-created` | Fired when a relation is first established |
| `relation-joined` | Fired when a remote unit joins the relation |
| `relation-changed` | Fired when relation data is updated by a remote unit |
| `relation-departed` | Fired when a remote unit leaves the relation |
| `relation-broken` | Fired when the relation is fully removed |

## Storage Hooks
*(fired per storage entry defined in `metadata.yaml`)*
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `storage-attached` | Fired when a storage volume is attached to the unit |
| `storage-detaching` | Fired when a storage volume is about to be detached |

## Container / Pebble Hooks (Kubernetes sidecar charms)
*(fired per container defined in `metadata.yaml`)*
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `pebble-ready` | Fired when the Pebble agent in a workload container becomes ready |
| `pebble-custom-notice` | Fired when a custom Pebble notice is recorded in a container |
| `pebble-check-failed` | Fired when a Pebble health check starts failing |
| `pebble-check-recovered` | Fired when a Pebble health check recovers |

## Secret Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `secret-changed` | Fired on observer units when a secret's content is updated |
| `secret-expired` | Fired on the secret owner when a secret reaches its expiry time |
| `secret-rotate` | Fired on the secret owner when a secret reaches its rotation time |
| `secret-remove` | Fired on the secret owner when an old revision can be cleaned up |

## Series / OS Upgrade Hooks
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `pre-series-upgrade` | Fired before the machine's OS series is upgraded |
| `post-series-upgrade` | Fired after the machine's OS series is upgraded |

## Action Events
*(one per action defined in `actions.yaml`)*
| Hook | Description |
|---|---|
| `<action-name>` | Fired when an operator runs `juju run <unit> <action-name>` |

---

**Total: 27 distinct hook types** (plus one per relation endpoint, storage, container, and action you define). In the `ops` framework, these map to event names like `self.on.install`, `self.on.<relation_name>_relation_joined`, `self.on.<container_name>_pebble_ready`, etc.
